talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: USC Norris Comprehensive Cancer Center / Keck School of Medicine
talk_metadata.talk_title: Navigating Change and Maintaining Impact through Storytelling and AI
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We saw a 45% increase in the number of people who actually got an appointment or were already screened in the end of six months"
Speaker said: "We are now working with artificial intelligence and AI to really introduce elements that will facilitate the work that we're doing"

Speaker said: "We have to make sure that people identify, are transported, and really understand what the messaging is. Because the story of Sergio the beekeeper was so interesting that they did not hear anything that we were saying related to the information"

key_data_gaps: No quantitative outcome metrics beyond descriptive maps
No control group or randomized comparison for the AI‑generated storytelling interventions

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO's computational spine to generate AI‑assisted, health‑literacy optimized scripts and storyboards for community education, accelerating production beyond the current multi‑day process.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We utilize generative AI to draft simplified low health literacy scripts, converting complex clinical information into simplified, accurate, and easy‑to‑understand narratives"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can automate the generation and validation of low‑literacy scripts, reducing turnaround from weeks to hours while preserving cultural relevance.

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO's predictive modeling to identify sub‑populations within the catchment area that would benefit most from targeted screening (e.g., early‑onset colorectal cancer in Hispanic groups).
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted higher rates of colorectal cancer under age 50 in Hispanic populations and the need for targeted outreach.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate GIS, demographic, and genomic data to pinpoint high‑risk micro‑clusters for focused AI‑driven outreach.

cited_competitors.name: Genentech
cited_competitors.context: Funding source for the speaker's work

cited_competitors.name: National Cancer Institute
cited_competitors.context: Provides grant support and oversight for COE activities

unresolved_questions: How will the AI‑generated materials be rigorously evaluated for cultural accuracy and health impact?
What sustainable funding models can support the scaling of AI‑assisted storytelling beyond pilot projects?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Kentucky Markey Cancer Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: Building a Statewide Cancer Network to Expand Access and Research
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have 20 independently operated community hospitals, and 18 out of 20 are CoC accredited after joining the network"
Speaker said: "Since 2016, we've had over 1,400 accruals that have been enrolled at those sites"
Speaker said: "Advocates asked about non‑smokers and radon exposure, leading to a new paper on variant analysis"

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Markey Cancer Network community hospital trials
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Broad cancer care delivery across community hospitals
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all patients at community sites without molecular or biomarker pre‑screening for targeted therapies
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Lack of systematic ctDNA or genomic profiling at many community sites
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering of site‑specific outcome analyses due to heterogeneous patient mix

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Network reliance on external specialty centers for high‑complexity therapies
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted community hospitals cannot perform CAR‑T or advanced robotic surgeries, requiring referral back to Markey
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad network model may dilute signal for site‑specific clinical trial outcomes

key_data_gaps: No randomized comparison of network vs non‑network outcomes
Limited biomarker‑guided trial data from community sites

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to develop a centralized, AI‑driven biomarker triage pipeline that can be deployed to community hospitals lacking genomic testing capacity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that many community sites lack advanced molecular testing and rely on referrals for targeted therapies.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict likely actionable mutations from limited clinical data, prioritizing cases for full genomic profiling.

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement CrisPRO’s adaptive trial simulation tools to redesign network trials with stratified enrollment based on predicted response biomarkers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that community sites enroll patients without molecular pre‑screening, risking dilution of trial signal.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulation can model power and endpoint impacts of biomarker‑enriched cohorts before launch.

cited_competitors.name: NCTN
cited_competitors.context: National Cancer Institute trials referenced as part of the network’s portfolio

unresolved_questions: How will the network ensure consistent quality of molecular testing across disparate community sites?
What metrics will be used to evaluate long‑term impact of the hybrid care model on patient outcomes?

assets_to_track: Markey Cancer Network community hospital trial portfolio
companies_to_monitor: University of Kentucky Markey Cancer Center

talk_metadata.talk_title: Sustaining Community Engagement: Models, Funding, and Impact
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have trained over 160 community scientists, and they have given feedback over 200 times to researchers"
Speaker said: "100% said they felt that their feedback was valued and that it improved their research project"
Speaker said: "Around 90% of the implemented programs have been sustained because the city government has come on board to help fund these initiatives"

key_data_gaps: Lack of long‑term outcome data linking community scientist feedback to clinical trial accrual or patient survival

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO’s natural‑language processing to scale the evaluation of community scientist feedback across thousands of research proposals, ensuring consistent integration of lay perspectives.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted the volume of feedback (200+ instances) and the need to systematically incorporate it.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can auto‑summarize feedback themes and map them to protocol amendments, accelerating the loop between community input and trial design.

crispro_opportunity.description: Partner with MD Anderson’s Be Well initiative to embed CrisPRO‑generated, culturally tailored digital toolkits into community‑led health programs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described the Be Well initiative delivering evidence‑based actions (e.g., sun shades, community gardens) with high sustainability.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can personalize digital content to local demographics, enhancing adoption and measurable health impact.

cited_competitors.name: Bristol Myers Squibb Foundation
cited_competitors.context: Funding source for breast cancer disparity coalition

unresolved_questions: What quantitative metrics will be used to prove that community scientist feedback improves trial enrollment or patient outcomes?
How can funding models be diversified to sustain community engagement beyond philanthropic cycles?
